paragraphs: When the gods become vexed by civilizations, they do not send fire or flood—they choose corruptible families. Earth’s dysfunctional Shah family—Rashid, Jermaine, Sahil, Shahlini, Akeem, Sonam, and Priya—are drafted as deadly divine conscripts and granted power over mind and matter. Tasked with executing the gods' final judgment, they must each decide: remain tethered to a dying world or ascend as its conquerors.

But Rashid, the black sheep of the family, sees something the others don’t. Humanity may be broken, but it is still worth saving. Torn between the family he’s never fit into and a species on the brink of annihilation, Rashid chooses rebellion against his own family and the gods, putting targets on the backs of his closest friends.

With the world at stake, he embarks on a dangerous quest as he struggles to stay in control of a dangerous darkness that grows within. Through forged friendships, shared suffering, and defiant hope, Rashid begins to understand that family isn't always blood. Sometimes it's chosen.

As his family sheds their humanity and descends further into godlike wrath, Rashid faces a brutal question: how do you stop the end of the world when it wears the faces of those you love? A Family of Mortals is a tale of power, legacy, and the courage to stand alone against fate—even when it means standing against your own.
